CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-30-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Alex DDS Gutierrez., Gutierrez Alex DDS, and Gutierrez, Alex A. individual.

Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: R. Sahatjian. Reporter: Suzanne Onuk, CSR #13734. Telephone: 714-307-3241-9768. Email: Smonuki@yahoo.com

Atty. David Cook, Esq., appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc. No appearance by defendant

Witness: Kent Alleyne is present via Zoom who is sworn and examined.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Align Technology, and against the defaulted defendants Alex DDS Gutierrez., Gutierrez Alex DDS, and Gutierrez, Alex A. individual., as follows:

Damages/Principal - $98,100.68
Prejudgment Interest - $9,245.65
Attorney Fees - $1000.
Costs - $435.
Total - $108.781.33

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court (as modified). The Court will email a copy of the Order to Mr. Cook.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-01-2023 Continued To Aug-09-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Continued To Jun-30-23 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for notice of default prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Miranda L. Perez, CSR #14352; 949-599-5589; mirandalperez@outlook.com

Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a Delaware Corporation represented by David Julian Cook, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Alex A. Gutierrez, an Individual D.B.A. Gutierrez Orthodontics and Finestsmiles.com not appearing.

KEVIN ALLEYNE HENRY is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Plaintiff's Counsel examined re: proper venue by the Court.
Court requires evidence that San Francisco is the proper venue under the terms and conditions of the contract. Further, how the terms and conditions of the contract address attorney fees and interest.

After passing of the case, the Court determines Plaintiff does not have a copy of the Contract Agreement. Additionally, the amount sought to recover from Defendant is only noted in the title of Plaintiff's Complaint and in Exhibit A to the Complaint.

Court orders matter continued to June-30-2023 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 505 for further hearing. COURT ORDERS THE FOLLOWING:

1. Plaintiff shall file evidence to support damages sought by Plaintiff against Defendant and submit a copy of the Contract Agreement.
2. Plaintiff shall file evidence to show California is the proper venue and demonstrate why San Francisco was chosen as the forum.
3. Plaintiff shall file and serve Statement of Damages with supporting evidence.
4. Planitiff shall file a Declaration which includes its efforts and confirm compliance with the Court's orders.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Continued To May-24-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-23-2023 Continued To Apr-14-23 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 505
